question.text:
CASE STUDY (continued): Mr. Rajesh holds 10,000 units of the "Sunrise Equity Growth Fund" with NAV of Rs. 9.87 (as computed). He invested 8 months ago and the scheme charges an exit load of 1% for redemptions within 1 year.
Question 2 of 4: If Mr. Rajesh redeems all 10,000 units today, what is the total amount he will receive?

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Since Mr. Rajesh invested only 8 months ago (within 1 year), exit load of 1% applies. Repurchase Price = NAV - (NAV x Exit Load %) = Rs. 9.87 - (Rs. 9.87 x 1%) = Rs. 9.87 - Rs. 0.0987 = Rs. 9.7713. Total Redemption Amount = 10,000 units x Rs. 9.7713 = Rs. 97,713.
**The Trap:** Rs. 98,700 ignores exit load entirely (10,000 x Rs. 9.87). Rs. 97,000 rounds down too aggressively or uses a wrong calculation. Rs. 99,000 may come from applying exit load on face value (Rs. 10) instead of NAV. Exit load is always calculated on the applicable NAV at the time of redemption.
**Cross-Exam Context:** This case study question combines NAV computation with exit load application — a multi-step calculation typical of NISM V-A exams. The key steps are: (1) compute NAV, (2) apply exit load on NAV, (3) multiply repurchase price by units to get total redemption amount.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** In Mr. Rajesh's case, the exit load costs him Rs. 987 (Rs. 98,700 - Rs. 97,713). If he waits 4 more months to complete 1 year, he avoids this charge entirely. Always advise clients to check their exit load period before redeeming. A few months of patience can save meaningful amounts, especially on larger investments.
